100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 25, 1926, the British Empire showcased its strength as King George V opened the Empire Exhibition in Scotland, celebrating the unity and resources of its vast territories. Meanwhile, in the United States, the Supreme Court upheld the 1925 New York state law regulating the sale of milk, emphasizing public health amid growing concerns over food safety. Across Europe, unrest continued in Germany as political factions clashed, reflecting the ongoing instability in the Weimar Republic.
Updated: Thursday, June 25, 2026 at 08:52:52 AM CDT
